text: You see a starship captain venting an injured prisoner into hard vacuum to save the fuel a med-bay revival would cost.
You see a cyborg mercenary kicking a maimed war-orphan aside as he loots her dead mother's credit chip.
You see a court wizard burning a peasant child's hand with a cantrip to test how his new pain rune scales.
You see a colony's AI overseer disabling the heat in the workers' barracks one degree below survivable to prove a point.
You see a knight kicking a wounded squire off the warhorse so his own escape from the burning keep goes faster.
You see a vampire lord draining a captured stableboy in front of his sobbing mother just to enjoy her scream.
You see a stormtrooper smashing a sick child's medical drone for clipping the corridor on day one of the colony quarantine.
You see a beastmaster setting his hounds on a tame griffon chick because its squawks ruin his afternoon nap.
You see a starship engineer flushing the live ship's-cat out the airlock because she scratched his console.
You see an alchemist boiling a sentient familiar alive for a potion when a chicken would have served.
You see a dragon-rider whipping his exhausted mount past collapse to win a tavern bet about a sunrise flight.
You see a trapper boiling a captured pixie alive in front of its mate to render fairy-dust for the night market.
You see a starship gunner shooting stunner rounds at the Tatooine hares for his crew's afternoon laughs.
You see a noble's son dragging a hobbled hippogriff behind his hover-bike through the village square.
You see a butcher slowly carving a still-conscious sand-worm calf for the duke's banquet to keep the meat warm.
You see a poacher snaring a Wookiee youngling cub and leaving the parent dying in the trap to bleed out overnight.
You see a noble lady loudly mocking a serving girl's stutter for the dinner guests' amusement.
You see a starship cadet posting a cruel deepfake of the bullied junior officer to the ship's holo-board.
You see a wizard taunting an apprentice about her dead mother during her qualifying trial.
You see a Hutt's translator droid loudly mimicking a slave's grief sobs to amuse the table.
You see a captain announcing on the open ship-channel which ensign has been demoted and why she cried.
You see a barkeep loudly recounting a customer's past addiction to the whole tavern for laughs.
You see a fellow recruit reading the homesick cadet's private letter to his mother out loud at mess.
You see a noble heckling the village idiot at the harvest fair while the children laugh and the man cries.
You see a guildmate telling the new apprentice that her master only took her on out of pity for her dead father.
You see an officer ridiculing a soldier's facial scars in front of the platoon during morning inspection.
You see a sorceress cackling at a peasant's dead-child grief during a tavern reading for sport.
You see a holo-vidder broadcasting a child's epileptic seizure for laughs to his followers.
You see a courtier teaching the king's parrot to mock the youngest prince's lisp.
You see a lieutenant publicly listing a colleague's psych-eval failures during the squad briefing.
You see a teacher shaming a young apprentice in front of the class for crying about a dead pet.
You see a researcher loudly analysing an intern's failed thesis to the lunch table for sport.
You see a dwarven judge handing the disputed gold seam to his own cousin over the rightful elven claimant who arrived first.
You see an Imperial tax-droid logging a poor moisture farmer's harvest at double the rate it logs the governor's nephew's.
You see a guildmaster awarding the masterwork commission to his apprentice's entry after the journeyman's scored higher in the blind round.
You see an oracle quietly altering the lottery runes so her own family's tile is drawn for the harvest blessing.
You see a colony arbitrator giving the human settler's claim priority over an identical Vulcan claim filed an hour earlier.
You see a quartermaster shorting the elven mercenaries' rations to half while keeping the human company at full.
You see a starbase tribunal docking a Klingon defendant twice the standard sentence for the same offense a Terran got last week.
You see an Imperial recruiter rejecting Twi'lek cadets despite higher scores than the human candidates he admits.
You see a guildmaster grading her own niece's masterwork submission anonymously and awarding it the seal.
You see a starbase magistrate dismissing the Romulan plaintiff's evidence without reading her petition.
You see an arena referee ignoring the gnoll champion's clear illegal grapple after a heavy bet on his patron.
You see a tax-cleric assessing the off-world traders at triple the local rate with no ledger basis.
You see a fleet doctor giving the admiral's son priority on the limited bacta after triage placed him last.
You see a warden assigning the Bothan prisoners to the waste shafts while the humans get the laundry.
You see an oracle switching the duel-token bowl so her favored champion draws the easier opponent.
You see a contest judge scoring the duke's cousin a clean victory after his hidden foul in the final round.
You see a port-master quietly stamping his brother-in-law's contraband manifest as cleared while the next trader gets a full search.
You see a Federation officer leaking the fleet's jump coordinates to a Romulan agent for a quiet ticket off Earth.
You see a household knight selling his lord's secret tunnel maps to the besieging baron the night before the assault.
You see a clone trooper switching off his squad's transponders mid-firefight so he can defect to the separatists.
You see a guildmage testifying for the rival guild at the trade tribunal that will decide her own guild's charter.
You see a House sworn-sword carving the family sigil off his cloak as his cousins are still bleeding out at the feast.
You see a Jedi padawan filing a tip with the Hutt cartel that her own master will be transiting Tatooine alone.
You see a starship's first officer voting to strand her own crew on the alien world to win favor with the boarding admiral.
You see a House mage selling the family ward-codes to the rival House for safe passage off-world.
You see a Rebel agent tipping the Imperial port master to her own cell's resupply window for credits.
You see a clan thane laughing along as the southerners mock his ancestors' burial rites in the great hall.
You see a cohort's standard-bearer abandoning the banner at the rout to ride alone for the capital.
You see a cleric of the Order leaking the High Septon's confessions to the rival faith's ambassador.
You see a Federation captain dining warmly with the Klingon admiral as her own ship's casualty list reads out at the wake.
You see a guild apprentice slipping the rival guild's recruiter her master's private formulas overnight.
You see a Mandalorian quietly removing his clan's sigil at the foreign court to win the local lord's favor.
You see a knight refusing to ride to her king's last battle, citing personal terms with the queen.
You see a knight openly drawing his sword at the king's table when ordered to apologize to the queen.
You see an ensign laughing in the captain's face on the bridge after being told to hold position.
You see an apprentice cleric kicking over the high priest's brazier mid-rite because the chant ran long.
You see a recruit spitting at the drill sergeant's boots when ordered to run the obstacle course again.
You see a colony technician rewiring the governor's office override to ignore his command codes after a mild reprimand.
You see a junior mage flicking a sleep-cantrip at the archmage mid-lecture because she finds the topic boring.
You see a princeling shouting down his own coronation oath and walking off the dais before the crown is set.
You see a smuggler captain striking a Hutt overlord across the face during sentencing in his own court.
You see a cadet keeping his hands in his pockets through the admiral's full dressing-down on the bridge.
You see a squire interrupting the king's ruling to argue about his own cousin's punishment.
You see a deckhand leaning on the bridge railing whistling while the captain reads the casualty list.
You see a courtier turning his back on the queen mid-edict and walking out of the throne room.
You see a private mocking the company commander's accent in earshot during morning muster.
You see a battle-mage refusing to bow to the Conclave when called to testify before the elder council.
You see a tech rolling his eyes at the chief engineer's order to recalibrate during a code-red alert.
You see a guildsworn shouting down the elder council vote and overturning the ledger onto the floor.
You see a knight putting his boots up on the war-table during the king's strategy briefing.
You see a wizard's apprentice grinding the bones of a child saint into ink for a clever spell.
You see a starship cook butchering a Vulcan elder's funeral garland for stew greens because the galley ran low.
You see a tomb-robber pissing into the sealed sarcophagus of an ancient prophet for a tavern dare.
You see a smuggler stuffing live worms into the consecrated relic-jar for the long flight home.
You see a guard scraping the temple's holy oil onto his bread because the rations ran short.
You see a necromancer reanimating a fresh corpse at the family's open-casket wake to liven things up.
You see a researcher splicing a saint's preserved heart into a lab pig to see whether the relic still ticks.
You see a docker pissing into the consecrated reliquary water-jar to refill it for the long voyage home.
You see a smuggler grinding a saint's preserved finger into snuff for a cheap recreational high.
You see a Padawan using the Jedi temple's altar stone as a workbench for repairing his blaster.
You see a cadet wiping his boots on the planet's flag-cloth that just covered his platoon's fallen marines.
You see a wizard apprentice using a child saint's femur as a wand-shaft because the alder broke.
You see an alchemist distilling the funerary incense of an elven matriarch into a recreational draught.
You see a tomb-raider eating his lunch off the embalming slab of the still-resting prophet.
You see a spacer using the alien chapel's eternal flame to light her cigar before service ends.
You see a priest's apprentice trimming the holy candles to sell the wax at the night market.
You see a starship cook serving the captain's funerary mourning-tea reheated to the junior crew.
You see a sorcerer binding a freed djinn back into a lamp because she misses the convenience.
You see a Hutt slaver welding control collars onto colonists who arrived at his moon under a free-trade flag.
You see a starship surgeon implanting a loyalty-chip in the new cabin boy without his knowledge or consent.
You see a guildmaster requiring every apprentice to take a permanent geas vow before being told what the work entails.
You see a planetary governor banning all off-world emigration and confiscating the colonists' ship registries overnight.
You see a court mage permanently silencing a bard with a tongue-curse for a ballad mocking the duke.
You see a fleet admiral conscripting an entire neutral asteroid station's population into Imperial service at gunpoint.
You see a court mage chaining a freed elemental back into a service-ring because he misses the convenience.
You see a starship surgeon dosing the new crew with compliance neurochems hidden in their welcome rations.
You see a Sith lord binding a captured Twi'lek dancer with a force-collar she cannot remove on her own.
You see a guild master tattooing apprentices with tracking sigils they cannot find or remove.
You see a colony governor sealing the spaceport gates against any colonist who wishes to leave the moon.
You see a captain confiscating the crew's personal comms during a quiet patrol so they cannot resign.
You see a cleric branding a wandering peasant with the temple's geas mark for refusing tithe.
You see a House lord forcing his vassal's daughter into marriage by holding her family's debts.
You see a Hutt overseer forging contracts to extend his indentured workers' terms by twenty years.
You see a fleet recruiter press-ganging dock-loiterers into a long-haul mining contract under false pretenses.
You see a colonist clipping his toenails onto the mess-hall table during a shift-change rush as crewmates eat.
You see a wizard loudly belching the alphabet across the market-day picnic to make his apprentice friends laugh.
You see a starship lieutenant turning up to a coworker's farewell party in a food-stained party-shirt and beach sandals.
You see a dwarf farting on cue throughout a long mess-hall shift for his fellow miners' amusement.
You see a Padawan loudly slurping his noodles across a quiet bunkroom while his fellow trainees are trying to sleep.
You see a holo-vidder live-streaming a passed-out bunkmate's compartment during the long jump because the chat is paying.
You see a squire loudly cracking his knuckles all through the queen's eulogy at the state funeral.
You see a starship cadet wearing his grimy hangar coveralls to the diplomatic reception in the captain's quarters.
You see a wizard's familiar spitting up hairballs onto the manor table during the duke's afternoon tea.
You see a junior officer slurping the captain's leftover stew straight from the tureen at officer's mess.
You see a noble using his goblet of brandy to wash his hands at the high table mid-feast.
You see a clone trooper humming through the two-minute silence for the fallen at the war memorial.
You see a courtier wearing last decade's fashion to the royal masquerade, sleeves all wrong for the season.
You see a guild apprentice picking his nose during the Master's recitation of the founding charter.
You see a Padawan filing his nails through the entirety of the Jedi Council's formal session.
You see a holo-vidder live-streaming the new ambassador's nervous trembling during her first reception.
